content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

April 5, 1983


Assemblyman Ross Johnson
State Capitol, Rm. 4130
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn: Penny Williams

Re: Our Advice No. A-83-092

Dear Assemblyman Johnson:

     I am writing in response to your recent question to
documents examiner Alice Hughes concerning whether or not
reimbursements of expenses, or repayments of loans, by a
campaign committee should be reported as income on a Statement
of Economic Interests. As a general rule, any "payment
received" is considered income under the Political Reform Act,
Government Code Section 82030,1/ and may have to be reported
on a Statement of Economic Interests. Section 82030 provides
some exceptions to this rule (none of which are relevant to your
question). The Commission has established some additional
exceptions for certain limited categories of payments from
campaign committees when reporting those payments would not
serve any purpose under the Act.

     In an Opinion requested by Assemblyman Willie Brown, Jr.,
1 FPPC 67 (1975), the Commission ruled that a candidate does not
need to report on his Statement of Economic Interests the
reimbursement of his campaign expenses by his controlled
committee. The Commission reasoned that, since the candidate
must disclose the reimbursement on his committee's campaign
statement, which he must personally verify, no purpose would be
served by additional disclosure of the reimbursements on his

_________________
1/ All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise stated.

Assemblyman Ross Johnson
April 5, 1983
Page Two


Statement of Economic Interests. In accordance with this
opinion, and with Section 82030, payments to a public official
from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows:

     1. Reimbursement of an official's expenses, or direct
payments for the official's expenses,2/ by that official's
controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's
Statement of Economic Interests.

     2. Repayment of an official's expenses, or direct payment
for an official's expenses, by a committee which is not
controlled by the official must be reported on the official's
Statement of Economic Interests.

     3. Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public
official from his personal funds to that official's controlled
committee need not be reported on the official's Statement of
Economic Interests. Payment of interest on the loan by the
controlled committee must be reported, however.

     4. Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made
by a public official to a committee which is not controlled by
the official must be reported on the official's Statement of
Economic Interests.

     If you have any further questions about your Statement of
Economic Interests, please contact Alice Hughes at 916/322-6444.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief, Legal Division

BAM:km
_______________________

     2/ Of course, these expenses must be campaign related or
have a governmental purpose. Elections Code Sections
12400-12407.

sections.conclusion: Payments to a public official from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows: 1. Reimbursement of an official's expenses, or direct payments for the official's expenses, by that official's controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's Statement of Economic Interests. 2. Repayment of an official's expenses, or direct payment for an official's expenses, by a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests. 3. Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public official from his personal funds to that official's controlled committee need not be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests. Payment of interest on the loan by the controlled committee must be reported, however. 4. Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made by a public official to a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Should reimbursements of expenses or loan repayments made by a campaign committee to a public official be reported as income on the official's Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that reimbursements and loan repayments from a controlled campaign committee need not be reported on a Statement of Economic Interests, but payments from non-controlled committees must be reported. Interest payments on loans are always reportable, even from controlled committees.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is an FPPC advice letter (A-83-092) with clear question and conclusion sections. OCR quality appears good. Minor OCR artifacts noted (e.g., '&~.' in address) but do not affect content extraction.

QUESTION: ). 
The Commission has established some additional 
exceptions for certain limited categories of payments from 
campaign committees when reporting those payments would not 
serve any purpose under the Act. 
In an Opinion requested by Assemblyman Willie Brown, Jr., 
1 FPPC 67 (1975), the Commission ruled that a candidate does not 
need to report on his Statement of Economic Interes~s the 
reimbursement of his campaign expenses by his controlled 
committee. 
The Commission reasoned that, since the candidate 
must disclose the reimbursement on his committee's campaign 
statement, which he must personally verify, no purpose would be 
served by additional disclosure of the reimbursements on his 
!/ All statutory references made are to the Government 
Code unless otherwise stated. 


Assemblyman Ross Johnson 
AprilS, 1983 
Page Two 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
In accordance with this 
opinion, and with Section 82030, payments to a public official 
from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows: 
1. 
Reimbursement of an official's expenses, or direct 
payments for the official's expenses,2/ by that official's 
controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
2. 
Repayment of an official's expenses, or direct payment 
for an official's expenses, by a committee which is not 
controlled by the official must be reported on the official's 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
3. 
Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public 
official from his personal funds to that official's controlled 
committee need not be reported on the official's Statement of 
Economic Interests. 
Payment of interest on the loan by the 
controlled committee must be reported, however. 
4. 
Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made 
by a public official to a commmittee which is not controlled by 
the official must be reported on the official's Statement of 
Economic Interests.

CONCLUSION: Payments to a public official from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows: 1. Reimbursement of an official's expenses, or direct payments for the official's expenses, by that official's controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's Statement of Economic Interests. 2. Repayment of an official's expenses, or direct payment for an official's expenses, by a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests. 3. Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public official from his personal funds to that official's controlled committee need not be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests. Payment of interest on the loan by the controlled committee must be reported, however. 4. Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made by a public official to a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported on the official's Statement of Economic Interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Assemblyman Ross Johnson's question about whether campaign committee reimbursements and loan repayments should be reported on Statements of Economic Interests. The Commission established four categories of disclosure rules based on whether the committee is controlled by the official and whether payments constitute principal or interest.
